title: Providers, version pinning, and the lock file
description: Providers translate HCL into provider API calls; unpinned provider/module versions make plans non-reproducible across runs and machines.
body: Use when a plan differs between machines or breaks after an upgrade. Providers are versioned plugins; the .terraform.lock.hcl records exact provider versions and hashes so every run resolves the same plugin. Loose version constraints let a new provider release change defaults or schema unexpectedly. Do not leave provider versions unconstrained on shared infrastructure. Actions: set explicit version constraints, commit the lock file, and upgrade deliberately with terraform init -upgrade plus a review. Validation signals include identical provider versions across CI and local and a clean plan after upgrade. Risk boundary: an accidental major-version provider bump can rewrite resource defaults; treat provider upgrades as reviewable changes.
